zh.Hertford, Hertfordshire, England: 英國賀特福爾賀特福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Clare於1185年出生在英國賀特福爾賀特福德時，她的父親Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford32歲，母親Lady Amice FitzWilliam25歲。她和Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。

zh-hans.Hertford, Hertfordshire, England: 英国賀特福爾賀特福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Clare于1185年出生在英国賀特福爾賀特福德时，她的父亲Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford32岁，母亲Lady Amice FitzWilliam25岁。她和Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Clare was born in 1185, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford, was 32 and her mother, Lady Amice FitzWilliam, was 25. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Clare est née en 1185, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Amice FitzWilliam, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Clare 1185 in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford 32 und ihre Mutter Lady Amice FitzWilliam 25. Sie hatte mit Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Clare è nata il nel 1185 nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Lady Amice FitzWilliam, aveva 25 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan.

ja.Hertford, Hertfordshire, England: イギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Clareが1185年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードで生まれた時，父Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertfordは32歳，母Lady Amice FitzWilliamは25歳であった。彼女はRhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychanとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Clare(이)가 1185년 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Amice FitzWilliam(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Clare nasceu em 1185, em Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lady Amice FitzWilliam, tinha 25 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Clare родилась в 1185 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Amice FitzWilliam, 25. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Clare nació en 1185, en Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Clare 3rd Earl of Hertford, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lady Amice FitzWilliam, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Rhys Gryg Lord Dynevor Fychan.
